content:
# Decay Mathematics — Decisions (March 2026)

## Decay Function: Power-Law
`D(t) = (1 + t_days)^(-α)` where t = days since creation (not last access).
Fat tail ensures Cold entries retain nonzero scores for reversible promotion.

## Alpha Values (per memory type)
Structural=0, Procedural=0.10, Decisional=0.15, Analytical=0.30, Episodic=0.60

## Composite Retention Score: Additive
```
retention_score = 0.35 × D(t, α_type) 
               + 0.30 × A(access_count, days_since_access)
               + 0.20 × G(inbound_edge_count)
               + 0.15 × C(confidence)
```

### Component definitions
- D(t) = (1+t)^(-α) — power-law, creation time, range [0,1]
- A = (log(1+count)/log(1+100)) × (1+days_since_access)^(-0.3) — range [0,1]
- G = min(inbound_edges / 10, 1.0) — inbound only, range [0,1]
- C = confidence if set, else 0.7 default — range [0,1]

### Key properties
- Additive, not multiplicative: access/graph can rescue old entries from decay
- New entries start Warm (~0.45-0.50), must earn Hot through access or connections
- Session affinity boosts are retrieval-layer, not tier-layer
- Weights: decay 35%, access 30%, graph 20%, confidence 15%

## Tier Thresholds (with hysteresis)
- Demotion: Hot→Warm < 0.50, Warm→Cold < 0.25, Cold→Forgotten < 0.05
- Promotion: Cold→Warm >= 0.35, Warm→Hot >= 0.60
- Gap of 0.10 prevents oscillation
